priorities: Aligning capital with philanthropic impact (reforestation, youth education)
Holding companies to proactive environmental leadership
redLines: Investments in companies that treat nature as a free resource
Any exposure to palm‑oil linked deforestation or shell‑company supply chains
Green‑washed ESG claims without verified supply‑chain data
preferredSectors: Land‑acquisition and restoration projects (e.g., Atlantic Forest)
Sustainable agriculture and eco‑friendly consumer goods
Firms with measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous‑land protection programs
avoidedSectors: Conventional palm‑oil producers and their downstream users
Industries with opaque or high‑risk supply chains (e.g., unscreened commodity traders)
Companies lacking verifiable ESG or biodiversity metrics

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term (10+ years), aligned with multi‑generational philanthropic goals
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low immediate liquidity needs; prefers dividend sweeps to foundation and reinvests cash into core defensive strategy

lifeEvents.description: Initial onboarding; wealth originates from a successful sustainable‑agriculture exit and is framed as an extension of their philanthropic mission.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Set the overarching ESG‑centric mandate and defined sustainability as the core investment lens.

lifeEvents.description: Capital call of USD 250,000 for a land‑acquisition project in the Atlantic Forest; funded by trimming low‑scoring ESG fixed‑income positions.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reallocated assets toward direct conservation assets while preserving the core sustainable equity strategy.

lifeEvents.description: Elena’s field trip to the Amazon reinforced the client’s preference for companies that invest in measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous protection.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened bias toward firms with on‑the‑ground restoration projects and tangible impact metrics.

lifeEvents.description: Sub‑account created for the foundation’s youth educational program, emphasizing capital markets as a tool for ecological restoration.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Added a dedicated allocation for education‑linked impact investments.

lifeEvents.description: Transition to new Relationship Manager Sarah Meier; client stressed need for continuity of values‑led investing.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandated strict adherence to existing biodiversity and transparency criteria under new stewardship.

lifeEvents.description: A major consumer‑goods conglomerate announced a zero‑deforestation palm‑oil policy and a multi‑million‑dollar reforestation foundation.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential new investment candidate that meets the client’s red‑line criteria and could be added to the portfolio.

personalProfile.interests: sustainable agriculture, rainforest restoration, biodiversity conservation, youth environmental education
personalProfile.quirks: prefers direct impact investments over generic products; values transparent, data‑backed ESG reporting
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of the Atlantic Forest project, updates on youth education program, concrete reforestation milestones
personalProfile.kids' education: supports foundation’s youth educational program focused on ecological stewardship

keyQuotes: "I want to actively penalize companies that treat nature as a free resource."
"We expect Sarah to bring the same alignment of values and environmental transparency to our portfolio that Thomas did. No standard, generic investment products."
"If a company takes a brave, absolute stance against palm oil deforestation and actively puts money into reforestation, that is exactly where my money should be."
"Companies that don't protect their supply chains from ecological collapse won't survive the next decade anyway."
"We view ourselves as progressive stewards of wealth."
